name: Haiku (Neural Networks for JAX by DeepMind)

performance.notes: JAX JIT-compiled; FLOP-for-FLOP comparable to PyTorch

agent_readiness.notes: Neural network library for JAX (DeepMind's Sonnet-like API for JAX). import haiku as hk. class MyNet(hk.Module): def __call__(self, x): return hk.Linear(10)(x). def forward(x): return MyNet()(x). net = hk.transform(forward). params = net.init(rng, x). y = net.apply(params, rng, x). hk.Linear, hk.Conv2D, hk.LSTM, hk.MultiHeadAttention, hk.BatchNorm. Stateful: hk.transform_with_state for BatchNorm state. hk.experimental.lift for nested modules. Checkpointing via orbax. Works with optax for optimization. Functional approach: separate params from computation — JAX-native. hk.next_rng_key() for random. hk.get_parameter() for custom params. Alternative to Flax (which uses Linen API with different ergonomics).

gotchas: Functional programming model — params are explicit, not implicit (different from PyTorch)
transform() vs transform_with_state() — easy to use wrong one for stateful modules
Less community than Flax or PyTorch Lightning
DeepMind's research focus — may prioritize research over API stability
JAX ecosystem knowledge required — not a standalone framework
best_when: JAX-based neural networks wanting DeepMind-style module API
Research teams following DeepMind's published codebases
When Equinox's PyTree approach is less preferred than Haiku's functional style
Transformers and attention mechanisms in JAX
avoid_when: Teams new to JAX (steep learning curve)
Production serving (Flax + Orbax more mature for deployment)
summary: Haiku is DeepMind's neural network library for JAX. hk.transform(fn); net.init(rng, x); net.apply(params, rng, x). hk.Linear, hk.Conv2D, hk.LSTM, hk.MultiHeadAttention. Functional: params explicit, not implicit. hk.transform_with_state for stateful modules (BatchNorm). Apache-2.0 by DeepMind. Less community than Flax. JAX functional paradigm. Best for JAX users following DeepMind research code. Equinox and Flax are more popular alternatives.
